channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Airtel Africa's Customer Base Surpasses 189 Million, Revenue Rises 31% in Q1
item.description: Airtel Africa reported a 31% revenue surge to KSh 239.93 billion in Q1 2026, driven by a 11.6% growth in its customer base to 189 million across Africa. Data users increased to 87.3 million, with netw...

item.title: Tanzania Needs Innovation and Leadership to Achieve $1 Trillion Economy Goal by 2050
item.description: The article discusses the urgent need for Tanzanian public institutions to move away from traditional practices and adopt innovative, accountable leadership to meet the country's economic goals. Minis...

item.title: Analysts Agree with Fox News on Trump's Negative Polling Impact
item.description: A recent Fox News poll has delivered devastating news to Republicans, leaving a panel of MS NOW experts stunned that they agreed with the conservative network's assessment of President Donald Trump. T...

item.title: Fire Destroys Part of Commercial Building in Lagos
item.description: An early morning fire broke out at a commercial building in the Akowonjo area of Lagos State, destroying parts of the structure. The incident occurred around Jimoh Bus Stop, Egbeda-Akowonjo, and invol...

item.title: Italy seeks extradition of Toronto man accused of mob ties
item.description: Carlo Bruzzese, a 41-year-old Toronto-area resident, faces potential extradition to Italy after being arrested by Canadian authorities. Italian prosecutors allege he is a high-ranking member of the Ca...

item.title: Saudi nuclear deal tied to Abraham Accords participation with Israel
item.description: US President Donald Trump stated that the proposed US-Saudi civil nuclear agreement will only be approved if Saudi Arabia normalises relations with Israel by joining the Abraham Accords. The deal, whi...

item.title: Manchester Synagogue Attack: Police Fail to Examine Suspect's Devices Before Tragedy
item.description: A senior police chief has apologised after admitting mobile devices seized from the Manchester synagogue attacker were not examined before the fatal attack. Jihad Al-Shamie, 35, was on bail for an all...

item.title: Deputy PM Ishaq Dar highlights global challenges at Asean forum, including Kashmir and IWT disputes
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister Ishaq Dar addressed the Asean Regional Forum (ARF) ministerial meeting, emphasizing Kashmir and the Indus Water Treaty (IWT) as critical global challenges threatening regional pe...

item.title: Youth Protests Against Exam Irregularities Gain Support From Celebrities And Public Figures
item.description: Youth protests against alleged irregularities in competitive exams have gained significant public attention, with actors, sportspersons, and politicians extending support. The movement began on June 6...

item.title: MAGA-Themed Pornography and Its Link to Immigration Crackdowns
item.description: The article explores the emergence of niche pornographic content on X (formerly Twitter) that blends MAGA ideology with white supremacist themes, often referencing ICE raids and immigration crackdowns...

item.title: President William Ruto's Pledges for Pregnant Women and Children Spark Mixed Reactions
item.description: President William Ruto's recent promises to support pregnant women and their children have sparked mixed reactions across Kenya. During a high-profile event at State House, Ruto addressed hundreds of ...

item.title: Canadian premiers demand plan to counter Trump tariffs amid trade tensions
item.description: Canadian provincial premiers are escalating pressure on Prime Minister Justin Trudeau's government to unveil a concrete strategy to mitigate the economic impact of U.S. President Donald Trump's propos...

item.title: Italy Considers Pep Guardiola as New Coach After World Cup Exit
item.description: The Italian football federation (FIGC) has initiated discussions with Pep Guardiola regarding a potential coaching role, following the nation's failure to qualify for the World Cup. This comes after G...

item.title: Corporate Acquisitions in Kenya Intensify Talent Competition Among Banks and Telcos
item.description: A significant increase in high-stakes corporate acquisitions across East Africa, particularly in Kenya, is intensifying competition for top executive talent among financial institutions and telecommun...

item.title: Ehiz Dadaboy's Radio Comeback Showcases Media's Vital Role in Nigerian Entertainment
item.description: A week-long 'Radio Is Dead' campaign by Lounge Network ended with the announcement of Ehiz Dadaboy (Okoeguale Ehizojie) as host of a new breakfast show on Lagos radio. The initiative aimed to challeng...

item.title: Legal Analyst Says Trump's Social Media Post Could Weaken Any Future Case Against Jack Smith
item.description: The article reports on comments made by CNN legal analyst Elie Honig regarding President Donald Trump's public response to House Judiciary Committee Chairman Jim Jordan's criminal referral targeting f...

item.title: Houthis Claim Attacks on Saudi Oil Tankers as U.S.-Iran Conflict Expands Across Regional Shipping Routes
item.description: The conflict involving the United States, Iran, and Iranian-backed groups widened as Yemen's Houthi movement claimed responsibility for attacks on two Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea, raising concern...

item.title: Iraq's Political and Economic Struggles Amid Conflict and Corruption
item.description: The article explores the complex political and economic challenges facing Iraq, highlighting its deepening conflict with Iran, the role of pro-Iranian militias, and systemic corruption that has plague...

item.title: President Marcos Inspects Malampaya Phase 4 Project to Boost Energy Security
item.description: President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. conducted an aerial inspection of the Malampaya Phase 4 Project off Palawan, emphasizing the administration's focus on enhancing energy security through domes...

item.title: Pakistan Calls for Global Scrutiny of Afghan Taliban's Terror Links
item.description: Pakistan has called upon Amnesty International and the international community to rigorously examine the Afghan Taliban regime's continued support for terrorist groups. This follows a report by Amnest...
